name: Ph.D. in Applied Physics

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The referenced courses form a recognizable, well-ordered graduate physics core: PHYS 611 Advanced Classical Mechanics, PHYS 621 Classical Electrodynamics feeding PHYS 721 Classical Electrodynamics II, PHYS 641 Statistical Mechanics, and PHYS 731 Quantum Mechanics II gated on PHYS 631, capped by the PHYS 791 Doctoral Seminar. The I-to-II sequencing in E&M and quantum gives a logical build, though no applied-specialization track is visible on the page.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a visible plan of study that carries the core into applied specialization tracks (condensed matter, photonics, quantum information) plus qualifying-exam and research milestones.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The prerequisite graph is sensible: deepestPrereqChain=1 with clean, discipline-appropriate gates (PHYS 721 requires PHYS 621; PHYS 731 requires PHYS 631). missingCourseCount=2 consists only of PHYS 790A and PHYS 792B, which are pre-doctoral research and dissertation courses that legitimately dangle against the undergraduate catalog, not broken instructional links.
dimensions.suggestion: Surface the full first-semester core (e.g., PHYS 631 Quantum Mechanics I) as explicit prerequisites so the II-series chain and qualifying-exam gating are visible rather than implied.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes, doctoral competencies, or milestone objectives appear in the data; the page presents only a course core, which is a real omission for a Ph.D. catalog page.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish Ph.D. learning outcomes and milestones (qualifying exam, candidacy/dissertation proposal, original-research and dissertation-defense competencies).

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits=null, requirementGroups=0, and creditReconcileDelta=null, so the total doctoral credit requirement and the split among coursework, research, and dissertation are entirely absent -- a genuine transparency gap for a Ph.D.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the total post-bachelor (and post-master) credit requirement and the coursework/research/dissertation credit distribution.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The core courses are present and the advanced ones are described well (PHYS 721 lists radiation, relativity, and scattering; PHYS 731 covers second quantization and the Dirac equation), but several excerpts are empty (PHYS 641, PHYS 791) or reduced to 'Restrictions: Permission of the course instructor' (PHYS 611, PHYS 621), and there is no plan-of-study layout.
dimensions.suggestion: Fill in the missing/thin course descriptions and add a program-of-study layout showing core, electives, and research phases.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The visible curriculum is the timeless theory core (mechanics, E&M, statistical and quantum mechanics), which is sound, but for an *Applied* Physics doctorate the page surfaces no applied or contemporary electives -- condensed matter, photonics, device physics, quantum information, or computational physics -- so applied currency is not demonstrated.
dimensions.suggestion: Surface applied electives (condensed-matter/solid-state, photonics/optics, quantum information science, device and computational physics) that distinguish an applied doctorate from a pure-physics one.

descriptionQuality.justification: Description quality is uneven: PHYS 721 and PHYS 731 carry substantive, specific content, but PHYS 611 and PHYS 621 are reduced to a permission/restriction note and PHYS 641 and PHYS 791 have empty excerpts, so half of the sampled courses lack informative descriptions.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Where prerequisites exist they are stated unambiguously and consistently (PHYS 721: 'PHYS 621 or equivalent; basic knowledge of tensor analysis'; PHYS 731: 'PHYS 631 or equivalent'), with no dangling instructional references -- the only missing codes are research/dissertation courses.

missingTopics.rationale: The visible core is entirely mechanics, E&M, statistical, and quantum theory; condensed matter is the backbone of applied physics and no such course is surfaced to complement PHYS 641 and PHYS 731.
Modern doctoral research is simulation-heavy, but no computational-physics course appears among the referenced courses to support the theory core.
Optics/photonics is a central applied-physics research and industry area; PHYS 621/721 electromagnetics could feed a dedicated photonics course that is currently absent.
An applied doctorate should include graduate experimental-methods/instrumentation training, which is not visible alongside the theory sequence.

emergingTrends.rationale: Quantum computing, sensing, and communication are the fastest-growing applied-physics directions and are a natural extension of the PHYS 631/731 quantum sequence.
Data-driven modeling and ML analysis of experimental and simulation data are now standard; a methods course would modernize the doctoral toolkit.
Engineered optical materials build directly on the classical-electrodynamics core (PHYS 621/721) and are a major research and device frontier.
Topological insulators and 2D quantum materials link the quantum and condensed-matter cores and are a defining current research area for applied physics.

topSuggestions: Publish the doctoral credit requirement and a plan of study (coursework vs research vs dissertation); statedTotalCredits is currently null.
Surface applied specialization electives: condensed matter, photonics/optics, quantum information, and computational physics.
Publish Ph.D. learning outcomes and milestone structure (qualifying exam, candidacy, dissertation).
Complete the missing/thin course descriptions (PHYS 641, PHYS 611, PHYS 621, PHYS 791).
Add a quantum information science course to extend and modernize the PHYS 631/731 quantum sequence.
